_meta.notice: Seed lexicon for Day 2 of the v0.1.0 build. Every entry's `source` MUST be verified against EUR-Lex by the regulator-validator agent before Day 3 rule modules consume these triggers. NO_DIRECT_SOURCE or FAIL findings from regulator-validator block Day 3 work.

1_biometrics.description: Annex III.1 — remote biometric identification, biometric categorisation by sensitive/protected attributes, emotion recognition.

2_critical_infrastructure.description: Annex III.2 — safety components of critical digital, road traffic, water, gas, heating, electricity supply infrastructure.

3_education.description: Annex III.3 — access/admission to educational institutions, evaluation of learning outcomes, assessing level of education, monitoring prohibited behaviour during tests.

4_employment.description: Annex III.4 — recruitment, selection, decisions affecting terms of employment, promotion, termination, task allocation, monitoring/evaluation of performance.

5_essential_services.description: Annex III.5 — public assistance eligibility, creditworthiness/credit scoring, life and health insurance risk assessment/pricing, emergency services dispatch.

6_law_enforcement.description: Annex III.6 — risk assessment, polygraphs, evidence reliability, profiling of natural persons, deep analytical crime data analysis.

7_migration_border.description: Annex III.7 — migration, asylum, border control: risk assessment, polygraphs, examining applications for visa/asylum/residence, detection/identification at borders.

8_justice_democracy.description: Annex III.8 — administration of justice (researching/interpreting facts and law, applying law to facts), influencing elections/referendums/voting behaviour.

a_subliminal_manipulative.description: Art 5(1)(a) — subliminal techniques beyond awareness, or purposefully manipulative or deceptive techniques materially distorting behaviour.

b_exploit_vulnerabilities.description: Art 5(1)(b) — exploiting vulnerabilities due to age, disability, or specific social/economic situation.

c_social_scoring.description: Art 5(1)(c) — evaluation/classification of natural persons over time based on social behaviour or personality characteristics (social scoring).

d_predictive_policing.description: Art 5(1)(d) — risk assessment to predict criminal offences based solely on profiling or personality traits.

e_facial_scraping.description: Art 5(1)(e) — untargeted scraping of facial images from the internet or CCTV to build facial-recognition databases.

f_emotion_in_workplace_education.description: Art 5(1)(f) — inferring emotions in workplace or education (with medical/safety exceptions).

g_biometric_categorisation_sensitive.description: Art 5(1)(g) — biometric categorisation to infer race, political opinion, trade-union membership, religious or philosophical beliefs, sex life, sexual orientation.

h_realtime_remote_biometric_le.description: Art 5(1)(h) — real-time remote biometric identification in publicly accessible spaces for law-enforcement (narrow exceptions only).

1_interaction_disclosure.description: Art 50(1) — providers of AI systems intended to interact directly with natural persons must disclose AI nature.

2_synthetic_content_marking.description: Art 50(2) — generative AI outputs must be marked in a machine-readable format as artificially generated.

3_emotion_categorisation_disclosure.description: Art 50(3) — deployers of emotion-recognition or biometric-categorisation systems must inform exposed persons.

4_deepfake_labeling.description: Art 50(4) — deployers generating or manipulating image/audio/video to constitute a deep fake must disclose artificial generation.

5_public_interest_text.description: Art 50(4) second sub-paragraph — deployers of AI systems generating text published to inform the public on matters of public interest must disclose artificial generation/manipulation.

research_only.description: Article 2(8) — research, testing, and development activity scope exclusion (pre-market). Surface for the rules engine to consider suppressing high-risk classification.

open_source_exemption.description: Recital 102-104 / Art 2(12) — free and open-source AI components have a narrow exception (does NOT apply to GPAI models with systemic risk or to high-risk systems). Flag for rules engine to handle nuance.

internal_use.description: Operational scope hint (internal / non-public deployment). NOT a regulatory exemption. The rule engine may use this as a soft signal to inform downstream Art 50 transparency obligations, but internal use does NOT exempt a system from Annex III high-risk classification or Article 5 prohibitions if otherwise triggered.
